reason: The Codex packet completed useful tracking work outside the parent loop. A later record-completion attempt hung in the generic wire-smoke gate with both reference and Rust servers listening, so this packet is recorded manually against focused evidence instead of claiming the broad smoke gate.
agent_summary: Implemented stateful CLIENT TRACKING ON/OFF, CLIENT CACHING, CLIENT GETREDIR, CLIENT TRACKINGINFO, BCAST prefix collision handling, runtime read tracking, common write invalidations, tracked BLMOVE wake invalidation, and tracking runtime reset on client reset.

wire_smoke_record_completion.note: record-completion hung in bash harness/oracle/smoke.sh --skip-build; the hung reference and Rust server processes were killed before this manual salvage.

remaining_failures: INFO does not emit tracking_total_items, tracking_total_keys, tracking_total_prefixes, or tracking_clients.
Script read-key tracking still misses some read-only command calls.
Broken redirect push behavior is incomplete when the redirect client terminates.
Switching from normal tracking to BCAST mode does not invalidate pre-BCAST keys exactly like upstream.
Some same-connection invalidation ordering around HDEL, MULTI/EXEC, eviction, FLUSHALL, and FLUSHDB still differs from upstream.
harness_learning: Large packets need declared secondary target files for dispatch/list hooks, otherwise the useful implementation lands outside the first packet's narrow target set.
Generic completion gates need hard timeouts around smoke runners so a useful packet cannot be wedged by a broad post-run gate.
